approved_control.objective: Perform implementation-level SOTA convergence for the HTTPS proxy contest task: audit the existing implementation, repair protocol and safety issues, add hard tests, fair benchmark evidence, and report revisions.
approved_control.what_counts_as_done: All required outcomes and result paths are directly evidenced; verification commands pass; final report claims are constrained to proven evidence; no required outcome is offset by another outcome; SOTA verified additionally requires the predeclared HTTPS proxy core matrix to pass the approved 20% hard threshold against a fair same-model libcurl-library baseline: throughput geometric-mean ratio >= 1.20; no predeclared core scenario below parity unless the requirements-approved benchmark method explicitly excludes it before measurement; both clients have zero protocol, TLS, proxy, and request errors; both clients use the same proxy/origin topology, TLS verification, mTLS material, request payload, concurrency, connection reuse, timeout policy, and response validation; the benchmark uses the real ylong_http_client public request path and a same-model libcurl-library baseline; causal/profile/ablation evidence rules out benchmark-only fast paths, disabled validation, startup overhead, pooling mismatch, and response-validation mismatch.

required_steps.transition: Current HTTPS proxy implementation is audited before patches are applied.
HTTPS proxy CONNECT handling follows required protocol semantics and fails closed on invalid responses.
Proxy TLS and proxy mutual TLS are verified end to end and kept distinct from origin TLS.
Sync and async proxy semantics are verified and accurately represented.
A contest-critical HTTPS proxy test matrix is implemented and run.
Benchmark methodology and claims are fair, reproducible, and correctly labeled.
README/report narrative is revised to match verified implementation and benchmark evidence.

Rebuild or rerun the fair same-model libcurl-library HTTPS proxy benchmark matrix and classify SOTA performance against the approved 20% threshold: throughput geometric-mean ratio >= 1.20, no mismatched baseline, and zero protocol/TLS/proxy/request errors.

Prove any passing approved 20% performance result uses the real ylong_http_client public request path and is causally explained by profiling, tracing, or ablation evidence rather than curl CLI startup overhead, pooling mismatch, disabled verification, missing mTLS, response-validation mismatch, or benchmark-only fast paths.
Run final runtime Counterexample Gate only after S8 and S9 pass the approved 20% SOTA threshold, real-client-path, fair same-model libcurl, and causal evidence gates.

invalidated_evidence: old gen-004 S9I/S9J unchanged hard-threshold strategy as current execution strategy
canonical fair matrix 0.991x throughput geomean as approved 20% SOTA proof
best retained runtime candidate 1.106x throughput geomean as approved 20% SOTA proof
S9H rejected/rolled-back one-row HTTP-only >1.20 result as current SOTA proof
any curl CLI-only or mismatched-baseline result as libcurl-library SOTA evidence
